title: myrsine novocaledonica subsp. boulindaensis
intro: Myrsine novocaledonica subsp. boulindaensis, commonly known as the Boulinda myrsine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is native to New Caledonia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the Boulinda myrsine can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
